flashcards.back: Big-O notation represents the upper bound or worst-case scenario of an algorithm's growth rate.
Time complexity measures the amount of time an algorithm takes to complete as a function of the input size.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between best-case and worst-case analysis?
flashcards.back: Best-case analyzes minimum requirements (optimal input), worst-case analyzes maximum requirements (least optimal input).

flashcards.front: Name three common complexity classes in increasing order of growth
flashcards.back: O(1) < O(log n) < O(n) < O(n log n) < O(n²) < O(2ⁿ) < O(n!)
Asymptotic analysis ignores constants and lower-order terms, focusing only on the growth rate as input size approaches infinity.
